title: phanes — Architecture (SSOT)
summary: Hosted autonomous-discovery SaaS — a generic autonomous-cycle platform: a tenant brings a measurable objective + verifier/oracle, phanes drives hexa-lang's OUROBOROS engine `hexa kick` and returns a verified, provenance-tracked result/catalog. Bound to https://phanes.dancinlab.org, but currently SERVING NOTHING: both container applications sit at max_instances=0 (container cost cutback), and every page request goes through the container, so all routes answer 'Failed to start container'. Switched off on purpose, not broken.
serve: python3 serve.py  (로컬 서버 + 브라우저 자동 오픈; file:// fetch 차단 우회)

tree.name: 🥚 phanes · 파네스
tree.role: Hosted autonomous-discovery SaaS — generic autonomous-cycle platform

children.name: overview
children.detail: Hosted autonomous-discovery SaaS — a generic autonomous-cycle platform.

children.name: A tenant brings a measurable objective + a
children.detail: A tenant brings a measurable objective + a verifier/oracle; phanes drives hexa-lang's OUROBOROS engine `hexa kick` (the loop goal → falsifier → saturation, rounds with a per-round honesty/falsification gate) against it and returns a verified, provenance-tracked result/catalog (their own private

children.name: echoes)
children.detail: echoes).

children.name: Phanes (Φάνης, φαίνω 'to bring to light') is
children.detail: Phanes (Φάνης, φαίνω 'to bring to light') is the Orphic primordial revealer entwined by the OUROBOROS — Ouro = the serpent (mechanism), Phanes = the light it reveals. Bound to https://phanes.dancinlab.org (containers capped at 0 — see the deploy node).

children.name: doc-note
children.detail: This JSON tree is the architecture SSOT (update-in-place, NOT append-only). Human viewer = ARCHITECTURE.html via `python3 serve.py`.

children.name: History/decisions narrated chronologically → CHANGELOG.jsonl
children.detail: History/decisions narrated chronologically → CHANGELOG.jsonl; governance/identity → CLAUDE.md; verifiable claims → CLAIMS.tape + .verdicts/<slug>/; cross-repo links → NEXUS.tape. This doc points only, never duplicates thresholds.

children.name: Migrated from scattered domain docs (DESIGN.md/DESIGN.log.md
children.detail: Migrated from scattered domain docs (DESIGN.md/DESIGN.log.md, GOAL.md/GOAL.log.md, ROADMAP.md/ROADMAP.log.md, PHANES.md/PHANES.log.md, INBOX.log.md, LATTICE_POLICY.md) retired 2026-06-18 → this tree + ARCHITECTURE.html viewer (hexa-codex/anima c4 standard).

children.name: status
children.role: OFF — bound to phanes.dancinlab.org, containers capped at max_instances=0
children.detail: Bound to https://phanes.dancinlab.org as a Workers Custom Domain (wrangler.jsonc routes[]). NOT serving: on Cloudflare phanes-phanesweb and phanes-phanesworker are both at max_instances=0 (set during the container cost cutback), and assets/ only covers web/static — every page request goes through the container, so all routes answer 'Failed to start container: Maximum number of running container instances exceeded'. The apex dancinlab.org now belongs to the main site (dancinlab/www — a container-free static Worker), so a container outage can no longer take the front door down. Reviving phanes = raise the caps (resumes container billing) then `bash deploy.sh` (colima Docker + the cloudflare.deploy.token secret). 2-tier design when running: phanes-phanesweb std-1 + phanes-phanesworker std-2 · data plane on R2 · dispatch on the phanes-jobs CF Queue.

children.name: downstream-discipline
children.detail: phanes consumes `hexa kick`; it does NOT fork the engine. Engine/platform gaps go upstream via ~/core/hexa-lang/inbox/patches/ (one concept per file). @D t_downstream_discipline.

children.name: migrated-from
children.detail: DESIGN.md/DESIGN.log.md (24 decisions) · GOAL.md/GOAL.log.md · ROADMAP.md/ROADMAP.log.md (P0–P6) · PHANES.md/PHANES.log.md · INBOX.log.md (ARXIV A4 map) · LATTICE_POLICY.md — retired 2026-06-18

children.name: 🎯 GOAL — the one sentence
children.detail: Complete hexa-lang's OUROBOROS cycle engine (`hexa kick`: goal → falsifier → saturation, round iteration + per-round honesty gate) as a GENERIC autonomous-cycle SaaS where a real company plugs in its own measurable objective + verifier and runs it over the web, receiving a verified,

children.name: provenance-tracked result/catalog
children.detail: provenance-tracked result/catalog — shipped small + honest + real like echoes-experience.

children.name: SSOT mirror of GOAL.md
children.detail: SSOT mirror of GOAL.md.

children.name: IS — scope B (the chain)
children.detail: The B-scope chain — five stages from the upstream engine to the per-tenant verified result.

children.name: hexa kick (upstream engine)
children.detail: hexa kick (OUROBOROS goal→falsifier→saturation, hexa-lang upstream, cycle h36 COMPLETE)

children.name: ★ pluggable seed + tenant verifier/oracle abstraction
children.detail: → ★ pluggable seed + tenant verifier/oracle abstraction (B's core NEW engine surface, upstream inbox/patches candidate)

children.name: phanes control plane
children.detail: → phanes control plane (auth · tenant isolation · job queue · metering)

children.name: phanes compute plane
children.detail: → phanes compute plane (Linux fleet · HEXA_VAL_ARENA=0 · per-job overlay)

children.name: per-tenant verified result/catalog
children.detail: → per-tenant verified result/catalog (provenance · falsifier audit trail · export)

children.name: = the web-ification
children.detail: . = the web-ification of 'a company safely runs anything through hexa kick given a measurable objective + verifier'.

children.name: NOT — what phanes is not
children.detail: NOT a fork of the kick engine (phanes is a downstream consumer; gaps — esp. the pluggable verifier — go to hexa-lang inbox/patches).

children.name: NOT a guarantee the objective is met (the
children.detail: NOT a guarantee the objective is met (the verifier signal is TENANT authority; phanes's honesty gate is advisory; the only hard stop is saturation/round-cap; over-claim forbidden — never claim 'met' without a tenant verifier PASS). NOT design-first (product decisions are fixed by decision gates).

children.name: NOT fake progress (a miss is recorded as
children.detail: NOT fake progress (a miss is recorded as a miss — g3 · LATTICE_POLICY).

children.name: 📐 DECISIONS (1–24) — the closed product + deployment gates
children.detail: Step-by-step decision gate (one user-confirmation per decision, never batched). Each = picked + rationale. SSOT for product decisions, folded from DESIGN.log.md. All product gates (1–6 + B-surface) and the deployment + Cloudflare migration gates (11–24) are CLOSED.

children.name: Supersede chains preserved
children.detail: Supersede chains preserved: 16 supersedes 4 · 21 supersedes 15+18 · 22 supersedes 11. Two falsifiers (F-D22, F-D23) RAN and PASSED on live infra.

children.name: D1 Product scope = B (generic cycle platform)
children.detail: Generic autonomous-cycle platform: company brings a measurable objective + verifier/oracle; phanes drives hexa kick's goal→falsifier→saturation loop and returns a verified, provenance-tracked result/catalog. Pluggable seed + tenant verifier. User directive over assistant rec (A).

children.name: B's genuine engine delta = a NEW pluggable
children.detail: B's genuine engine delta = a NEW pluggable seed+verifier abstraction over hexa kick (upstream inbox/patches candidate, not a fork). Honest-scope risk ACTIVE: the tenant-supplied verifier is the sole authority of record for 'objective met' (@D g_honest_scope.scope_b).

children.name: D2 Brand name = Phanes (파네스)
children.detail: Orphic primordial deity of revelation (φαίνω, 'to bring to light'), entwined by the OUROBOROS — a strict superset of hexa-lang's engine codename. Semantic precision over Palantir (φαίνω = actively bring hidden truth to light vs a passive seeing-stone). User directive '파네스 go'.

children.name: RISK ON RECORD (g3, user override)
children.detail: RISK ON RECORD (g3, user override): adjacent trademark collisions — Phanes Technologies (autonomous multi-agent AI) + Phanes Therapeutics (USPTO-registered TMs) + phanes.app. Mitigation: treat as project lore; formal USPTO class-42 clearance before any public launch (@D g_name_risk).

children.name: Manteia/Orrery/Mimir etc
children.detail: Manteia/Orrery/Mimir etc. recorded as alternatives.

children.name: D3 Deployment shape = 가+다 (demo funnel + dashboard)
children.detail: A public round-capped DEMO site (marketing funnel) AND a full authenticated DASHBOARD (job submit · result browser · catalog · billing). The job API is the SHARED SUBSTRATE beneath both, not a third product. User directive over assistant rec (나 API-only).

children.name: Honest constraint MANDATED (g3)
children.detail: Honest constraint MANDATED (g3): the public demo runs ONLY preset/curated objective+verifier scenarios, round-capped + sandboxed; arbitrary verifier submission is authenticated/paid-only (@D g_public_demo_constraint).

children.name: D4 GitHub org + remote = dancinlab/phanes (private)
children.detail: github.com/dancinlab/phanes — private repo, origin/main, scaffold pushed (38e5992). User directive. Private defers (not voids) the @D g_name_risk clearance obligation. dancinlab org matches the shipped echoes-experience pattern. SUPERSEDED BY D16 (repo goes public + source-available).

children.name: D5 License = 가 Proprietary / All Rights Reserved
children.detail: Proprietary, all rights reserved (© 2026 dancinlab). Short LICENSE committed; covers only phanes-original code, not the upstream hexa-lang components it invokes. Commercial private SaaS — code stays server-side; an OSS license would surrender the moat.

children.name: Simplest reversible default (proprietary → BSL later is
children.detail: Simplest reversible default (proprietary → BSL later is possible; un-MIT is not). BSL 1.1 kept as explicit fallback. UNCHANGED by D16 (public ≠ free-to-compete).

children.name: D6 Multi-tenant overlay isolation = 다 (hybrid)
children.detail: Per-job $HOME-jail/sandbox now (downstream, ships v1) + a parallel hexa-lang inbox/patch for a first-class HX_DATA_DIR (canonical path). Drop the $HOME-hijack once upstream lands; keep the per-job sandbox as defense-in-depth. Grounded in compiler/drill/checkpoint.hexa:53 (env('HOME')).

children.name: Two upstream handoffs filed
children.detail: Two upstream handoffs filed: phanes-hx-data-dir-per-tenant-isolation + phanes-pluggable-verifier-oracle-for-drill-loop. Concurrency sub-risk recorded (cmd_drill_batch serializes under a single $HOME).

children.name: D7 Service language/stack (P1) = POSIX shell substrate
children.detail: P1 substrate = POSIX shell (service/job_runner.sh + jobctl.sh) — the thinnest glue that IS the measured engine-invocation contract. P1b HTTP transport and later phases: hexa-native preferred (ecosystem hexa-first). Thin-slice discipline: a hexa HTTP server in P1 = a rabbit hole.

children.name: Reversible
children.detail: Reversible — P1b implements HTTP in hexa over the same jobctl semantics. The shell runner invokes the upstream hexa binary, never vendors/forks it.

children.name: D8 Frontend stack = 다 (HTMX + server-rendered HTML)
children.detail: HTMX 1.9.x + server-rendered HTML fragments from the hexa-native backend (service/http_phanes.hexa). No bundler, no SPA framework, no node toolchain. The backend is already hexa-native with stdlib/net; HTMX = exactly the topology it wants (server = single SSOT, browser swaps fragments).

children.name: Matches the dancinlab thin-client/smart-server tone (echoes,
children.detail: Matches the dancinlab thin-client/smart-server tone (echoes, wisp). Reversible (coexists with vanilla JS / future Svelte islands page-by-page). Measurement-gate: GET /dashboard returns HTML, POST returns a job-row fragment, HTMX poll terminates on done/failed.

children.name: D9 Multi-tenant switcher = a (re-auth switch)
children.detail: A 'switch tenant' control in the dashboard .dbar that destroys the session and redirects to /login (fresh GET re-prompts tenant + bearer token). POST /switch-tenant → session_destroy + redirect_with_cookie.

children.name: Honest to the real auth model (g3)
children.detail: Honest to the real auth model (g3): a session is intrinsically one (tenant, token) pair; each tenant has its own token; re-auth is the truthful UX. Minimal + secure (reuses the measured session_destroy + /login path); no session-format change; no cross-tenant token leak.

children.name: Reversible (a dropdown can be added later if
children.detail: Reversible (a dropdown can be added later if org-level SSO ever authorizes N tenants).

children.name: D10 Public demo result delivery = pre-computed/cached preset results
children.detail: The public /demo page displays pre-computed CACHED results of curated preset scenarios (each a real prior hexa kick run, labelled with provenance).

children.name: ZERO live kick on the unauthenticated surface
children.detail: ZERO live kick on the unauthenticated surface — /demo is pure server-rendered HTML from in-source constant data; never touches jobctl.sh, never spawns a process, never writes to the job store.

children.name: Strongest reading of @D g_public_demo_constraint
children.detail: Strongest reading of @D g_public_demo_constraint: no execution path = no abuse surface to rate-limit/sandbox. Still fully honest (g3): a cached result is a genuine prior run, not a mock; the CTA sends the visitor to /login to run their own objective.

children.name: HONEST SCOPE
children.detail: HONEST SCOPE: the 3 P4-v1 cached results are representative curated examples hand-authored from known mathematics (σ(6)=12, σ(28)=56, Euclid 2^(p-1)(2^p−1)), to be regenerated from real fleet job.json captures when P2.5 is live.

children.name: D11 Hosting target = AWS EC2 single Linux host (v1)
children.role: DECIDED — superseded by D22 for compute
children.detail: A single Linux host (control + compute co-located), t4g.small (2 vCPU Graviton arm64, 2 GiB). Cloudflare optional as front-proxy (DNS · free TLS · CDN · DDoS). PHANES_ENGINE_HOST abstraction retained so a later fleet/CF-Containers move is a config change.

children.name: EC2 runs as-built (persistent EBS = filesystem .store/
children.detail: EC2 runs as-built (persistent EBS = filesystem .store/ survives; always-on host completes nohup-ed kick). Satisfies the hexa-lang Axis-D constraint (production must not run Mac-native kick). Cost ~$9–14/mo. SUPERSEDED BY D22 (compute moves to Cloudflare Containers; AWS account no longer needed).

children.name: D12 Pricing model = 다 (tier + metered hybrid)
children.detail: A base tier with an included quota, plus metered overage. Each tier bundles a fixed number of OUROBOROS rounds per billing period; usage beyond the bundle is metered per round. The OUROBOROS ROUND is the billing unit.

children.name: A kick job burns real compute, so revenue
children.detail: A kick job burns real compute, so revenue must track cost; a base tier keeps it sellable (predictable floor); the metering substrate already exists (job.json records rounds + wall_ms — metering is aggregation, no new instrumentation).

children.name: The round is the honest billing unit (consistent
children.detail: The round is the honest billing unit (consistent with scope B — bill only measurably-performed work). HONEST SCOPE: tier names/prices/bundle sizes need a real EC2 per-round cost measurement first.

children.name: D13 Payment processor = 가 (Stripe)
children.detail: Stripe Billing for the base-tier subscription + Stripe Metering for per-round overage. Integrated by direct Stripe HTTP API calls (no SDK — phanes is hexa-native; stdlib/net http_client is the transport). Card data never touches phanes (Stripe-hosted Checkout → no PCI-DSS scope).
1:1 fit with the D12 hybrid (existing job.json round aggregation → one usage-record API call). Global default (vs a Korea-only PG or a higher-fee Merchant-of-Record).

children.name: HONEST SCOPE
children.detail: HONEST SCOPE: there is no Stripe SDK for hexa — hand-written direct HTTP API calls; real future ROADMAP work, not done by this decision.

children.name: D14 Deployment method = 나 (repo-committed deploy.sh)
children.detail: A deploy script committed to the repo: rsyncs source to the host, runs service/build.sh there, health-checks /v1/healthz, rolls back to the previous binary on failure. No IaC, no container image.

children.name: For one host (D11), IaC is over-engineering
children.detail: For one host (D11), IaC is over-engineering; a committed script is reproducible (manual SSH is not); a container image conflicts with D11's native-binary shape. Grows without rework (becomes the per-host step a fleet orchestrator calls).

children.name: HONEST SCOPE
children.detail: HONEST SCOPE: the actual deploy needs the user's AWS account (EC2 instance/key/SG are out-of-repo); the script covers 'given a host exists, ship to it.'

children.name: D15 Managed datastore = 가 (DynamoDB + S3)
children.detail: DynamoDB for structured records (sessions, tenants, job metadata) + S3 for overlay blobs (atlas.overlay.n6). Replaces the filesystem .store/ job store.

children.name: User directive 'db 는 managedDB.' hexa-native HTTP fit
children.detail: User directive 'db 는 managedDB.' hexa-native HTTP fit (HTTP/JSON API, same transport as Stripe — no Postgres wire driver needed); phanes access patterns are simple key/item lookups; serverless pay-per-request fits a zero-user start; fully managed.

children.name: SUPERSEDED BY D21 (ALL-R2
children.detail: SUPERSEDED BY D21 (ALL-R2: DynamoDB + S3 both dropped). Kept on record with the supersede note.

children.name: D16 Repository visibility = 나 (public + source-available)
children.detail: The dancinlab/phanes repo goes PUBLIC, with the proprietary/All-Rights-Reserved license RETAINED (source-available: code visible to all, no rights granted, may not be run as a competing service). SUPERSEDES D4 (private); D5 (proprietary license) UNCHANGED. User directive '정식을 팔자'.

children.name: phanes sells honesty, so its code should be
children.detail: phanes sells honesty, so its code should be auditable (public code makes @D g_honest_scope.scope_b verifiable by anyone = trust proof + sales asset); consistent with the public dancinlab ecosystem (hexa-lang, echoes, demiurge); source-available keeps D5's commercial moat (Sentry/GitLab-core model).

children.name: PRECONDITION EXECUTED
children.detail: PRECONDITION EXECUTED: web/refs/ third-party design snapshots git-rm-cached + gitignored (a public repo must not redistribute others' copyrighted assets; note: still in history at d5a4515 — history purge flagged pre-flip).

children.name: D17 AWS credentials = startup-wrapper + secret CLI
children.detail: AWS credentials loaded by a startup wrapper (service/run-phanes.sh) that calls the user's secret CLI, exports the standard AWS SDK env vars, then execs bin/phanes-http. Repo stays credential-free; phanes-http reads only the AWS env vars (tool-agnostic).

children.name: secret key convention (dot-separated)
children.detail: secret key convention (dot-separated): aws.phanes.access_key_id → AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID, aws.phanes.secret_access_key, aws.phanes.region (default us-east-1), aws.phanes.session_token (STS only). exec-style wrapper = no orphan shell; fail-clean on missing secrets.

children.name: Deployment
children.detail: Deployment: the systemd unit service/phanes-http.service points ExecStart at run-phanes.sh. (Largely mooted by D21/D22 dropping AWS — the R2 token replaces the AWS creds; the wrapper pattern carries over to r2.phanes.*.)

children.name: D18 DynamoDB table schema = single-table, on-demand
children.detail: A single DynamoDB table `phanes` with composite PK+SK, holding sessions/tenants/jobs as heterogeneous prefix-discriminated items; on-demand billing.

children.name: Layout
children.detail: Layout: Session PK=SESSION#<sid> SK=SESSION (+ttl); Tenant PK=TENANT#<name> SK=TENANT; Job PK=TENANT#<name> SK=JOB#<ts>#<id> (newest-first via reverse Query).

children.name: The one non-KV pattern (jobs newest-N per tenant)
children.detail: The one non-KV pattern (jobs newest-N per tenant) maps to a Query (no GSI/scan); native TTL retires session-GC; one table = one IAM/dashboard/backup. SUPERSEDED BY D21 (ALL-R2). Kept on record with the supersede note.

children.name: D19 Discovery absorption → hexa-lang atlas (direction pinned)
children.detail: PINNED DIRECTION (not yet implemented), per user directive 'kick 으로 발견되는 방정식들도 흡수해야되':

children.name: Direction
children.detail: validated discoveries phanes' OUROBOROS engine produces feed back UPSTREAM into hexa-lang's atlas, closing the loop ecosystem-wide (phanes = a discovery FEEDER, mirroring qrng/qmirror/sim-universe absorbs).

children.name: OPEN SUB-GATES
children.detail: OPEN SUB-GATES (19a opt-in policy — tenant data is theirs, scope B, no silent absorption; 19b eligibility — likely verifier-passed + saturated only; 19c pipeline — job result → absorption serializer → hexa-lang inbox/notes → review → atlas citation).

children.name: Depends + honest scope
children.detail: Depends on D18/D21 (structured discovery record). HONEST SCOPE: nothing about absorption ships yet.

children.name: D20 Domain & DNS = phanes.dancinlab.org on Cloudflare
children.detail: Production domain = phanes.dancinlab.org, DNS managed at Cloudflare (concretises the D11 front-proxy: CF sits in front of origin, terminating TLS, fronting the CDN, absorbing DDoS). The apex dancinlab.org is NOT phanes — it is the main site (dancinlab/www). A product does not own the front door: phanes is container-backed, and a container outage on the apex takes the whole org's front page down.

children.name: CF is $0 for the layer phanes needs
children.detail: CF is $0 for the layer phanes needs (DNS + free TLS + CDN + basic DDoS); operationally separates concerns (AWS = compute+data, CF = DNS+edge); TLS at the edge makes the session cookie's Secure flag practical; pre-positions a future CF-Containers move.

children.name: HONEST SCOPE
children.detail: HONEST SCOPE: brand+domain both spell 'dancinlab' (no extra i — earlier dancinliab.org was a typo); registration/NS/zone setup is the user's account-side action.

children.name: D21 Datastore = ALL Cloudflare R2 (supersedes D15 + D18)
children.detail: The entire datastore — overlay blobs AND structured records (sessions, tenants, jobs) — lives in Cloudflare R2 (S3-compatible).

children.name: Drops + supersede
children.detail: DynamoDB DROPPED, AWS S3 DROPPED. SUPERSEDES D15 + D18. User directive 'all r2'.

children.name: Resolves AWS-IAM friction
children.detail: Resolves the AWS-IAM friction: phanes makes ZERO AWS API calls; the only storage credential is a Cloudflare R2 API token (no AWS IAM user).

children.name: S3-compatible SigV4
children.detail: R2 exposes an S3-compatible SigV4 API (service='s3', region='auto', host <acct>.r2.cloudflarestorage.com) — the existing SigV4 signer (stdlib/aws/sigv4.hexa) works unchanged.

children.name: Object layout
children.detail: Object layout: sessions/<sid>.json · tenants/<name>.json · tenants/<name>/jobs/<reverse-ts>-<id>.json · overlays/<id>.n6; newest-N = ListObjectsV2 + client-side sort. R2 free tier + free egress.

children.name: HONEST SCOPE
children.detail: HONEST SCOPE: ListObjectsV2 client-side sort is slower than a DynamoDB Query (fine at low volume; a maintained _index.json is the future optimization).

children.name: D22 Compute host = 나 Cloudflare Containers, 2-tier (F-D22 PASS)
children.role: LOCKED — F-D22 PASS (supersedes D11 for compute)
children.detail: Cloudflare Containers (NOT EC2 — D11 superseded for compute; the AWS account is no longer needed).

children.name: Topology
children.detail: Topology: TWO container classes (web + worker) DECOUPLED through an R2-backed job queue, with the WORKER class horizontally scalable to N instances.

children.name: Why split + queue
children.detail: Web requests are ms; an OUROBOROS cycle is minutes+ — co-locating starves web latency / a container cap takes the web tier down / a redeploy kills in-flight jobs. Split + queue = zero-downtime web rolls while workers drain. Single provider with the datastore (D21 R2).

children.name: Falsifier F-D22 (measured)
children.detail: FALSIFIER F-D22 RAN + PASSED (measured, not reasoned): a real --rounds 5 kick measured wall=75.0s, peak RSS ≈ 4.02 GiB → standard-1 (4 GiB) is infeasible (OOM), standard-2 (6 GiB) is the WORKER FLOOR.

children.name: Two binding constraints
children.detail: Two binding constraints: (1) worker sizing floor = standard-2 (≥6 GiB), re-measure RSS before raising PHANES_ROUNDS_MAX; (2) worker must set sleepAfter > max job wall-time (containers#162) so a long kick is never sleep-killed.

children.name: Fallback not triggered
children.detail: EC2-worker fallback (D22') NOT triggered.

children.name: D23 Job queue = 둘 다 (R2 system-of-record + CF Queues dispatch signal)
children.role: DECIDED — R2 record + CF Queues pointer
children.detail: HYBRID — the two are different LAYERS, not competing stores.

children.name: R2 = system of record
children.detail: Cloudflare R2 = system of record (durable job state + artifacts as R2 objects, per D21).

children.name: CF Queues = dispatch signal
children.detail: Cloudflare Queues = the dispatch SIGNAL only: on submit the web tier writes the durable job record to R2 THEN sends a tiny {tenant, job_id} pointer message to a CF Queue; workers are queue consumers (woken on demand, no R2 list-poll latency), pull the full record from R2, run kick, write

children.name: status/overlay back
children.detail: status/overlay back.

children.name: The queue carries a pointer, never the data
children.detail: The queue carries a pointer, never the data.

children.name: Delivery semantics
children.detail: At-least-once + visibility timeout fits the idempotent-per-job_id kick model. FALSIFIER F-D23 (measured end-to-end before B3 locks).

children.name: HONEST SCOPE
children.detail: HONEST SCOPE: this reshapes the shell substrate (tenants/jobs are owned by jobctl/job_runner, not the clean hexa _kv_ seam) — a worker-tier rearchitecture, its own phase B3.

children.name: B3 progress
children.detail: B3 progress: R2 object CLI op landed (PHANES_R2_OP get/put/del/list); get/put/del round-trip exit-correct; LIST FAILED (SigV4 UriEncode/query-canonicalization punted in stdlib/aws/sigv4.hexa) → filed upstream (later resolved-ssot).

children.name: D24 Queue access model = a (CF Queues REST API, F-D23 PASS)
children.detail: CF Queues REST API direct from the hexa-native binary (NOT a Worker sidecar (b), NOT container-in-a-Worker (c)).

children.name: Why direct REST
children.detail: CF Queues exposes a full HTTP REST API (HTTP-push producer, HTTP-pull consumer, ack) so a non-Worker process does the whole lifecycle over the same signed-curl carrier phanes uses for R2.

children.name: Falsifier F-D23 (measured)
children.detail: FALSIFIER F-D23 RAN + PASSED (live CF): create queue → add http_pull consumer → send {tenant,job_id} → pull (body byte-EQ to sent) → ack (ackCount=1) → delete. The pointer message round-trips byte-exact from a non-Worker caller.

children.name: B3 full-chain closure
children.detail: B3 FULL-CHAIN CLOSURE measured end-to-end on live CF: producer http_phanes.hexa::q_send (3f7f728) · R2 job spec jobctl.sh (96790da) · consumer service/queue_worker.sh (REST pull → R2 GetObject → job_runner.sh one real kick → R2 PutObject terminal status → batch-ack).

children.detail: HONEST SCOPE: F-D23 used the global key; production must use a scoped CF token (pre-launch obligation).

children.name: 🏗 ARCHITECTURE — the live 2-tier topology + code surfaces
children.detail: The deployed shape (Decisions 20–24): Cloudflare edge (dancinlab.org) → web container (phanes-http, ms requests, sessions on R2) and worker pool ×N (kick / OUROBOROS, minutes), DECOUPLED through an R2-backed job queue + a CF Queues pointer signal.

children.name: Data plane = Cloudflare R2 (system of record
children.detail: Data plane = Cloudflare R2 (system of record + overlay blobs). Compute = Cloudflare Containers (web std-1×3 · worker std-2×5). Folded from PHANES.md / README structure + DESIGN.log.md deploy execution.

children.name: Edge — dancinlab.org / Cloudflare (D20)
children.detail: Cloudflare DNS · free TLS · CDN · WAF · /static cache, in front of the origin Worker. dancinlab.org bound as a Workers Custom Domain → the phanes Worker (cert provisioned). Mail records untouched (the cutover was additive).

children.name: Web tier — phanes-phanesweb (Cloudflare Container std-1 ×3)
children.detail: Stateless hexa-native HTTP server (service/http_phanes.hexa over stdlib/net).

children.name: path
children.detail: service/http_phanes.hexa

children.name: Served surfaces
children.detail: Serves a multilingual marketing site (cosmogony · Phanes · Demiurge · hexa-lang · Anima · Demo ·

children.name: Pricing · Contact), the public preset-only /demo (D10)
children.detail: Pricing · Contact), the public preset-only /demo (D10), the session-authenticated HTMX /dashboard (D8), and the job API (/v1/*).

children.name: Statelessness
children.detail: ms requests; sessions on R2 so instances are interchangeable.

children.name: On submit
children.detail: On submit: writes the durable job record to R2, then HTTP-pushes a {tenant, job_id} pointer to the CF Queue (q_send).

children.name: Worker tier — phanes-phanesworker (Cloudflare Container std-2 ×N)
children.detail: Stateless, interchangeable, horizontally scalable (D22 floor = std-2 / ≥6 GiB per F-D22).

children.name: path
children.detail: service/queue_worker.sh · service/job_runner.sh

children.name: Consumer loop (service/queue_worker.sh)
children.detail: A CF Queues consumer loop (service/queue_worker.sh): REST pull (scoped Bearer token) → R2 GetObject the full job spec → service/job_runner.sh runs ONE real hexa kick in a per-job $HOME-jail (HEXA_VAL_ARENA=0, seed validated, round/wall capped, optional tenant verifier sandboxed via env -i + timeout)

children.name: → R2 PutObject terminal status → batch-ack
children.detail: → R2 PutObject terminal status → batch-ack.

children.name: sleepAfter constraint
children.detail: Must set sleepAfter > max job wall-time (containers#162).

children.name: Job queue — phanes-jobs (CF Queue, pointer signal) + R2 record (D23/D24)
children.detail: R2 = system of record (durable job state + artifacts); the CF Queue carries a tiny {tenant, job_id} pointer only. http_pull consumer @600s. At-least-once + visibility timeout = the redelivery lease (kick is idempotent per job_id).

children.name: Reached over the CF Queues REST API from
children.detail: Reached over the CF Queues REST API from the non-Worker hexa binary (D24, F-D23 PASS byte-exact).

children.name: 📥 ARXIV A4 cross-pollination map (from hexa-lang ARXIV A4)

children.name: verify-able = 0 (honest, as expected
children.detail: verify-able = 0 (honest, as expected — PHANES is a systems/SaaS axis + OUROBOROS-engine consumer, so it has no closed-form hexa verify --expr atom; isomorphic to ANIMA A2, opposite to DEMIURGE A3 verify-native). A4 value = citation + cross-pollination.

Folded from INBOX.log.md. verdict (ASCII) = hexa-lang:ARXIV/.verdicts/arxiv-phanes-absorb/triage_a4.txt; docs = hexa-lang:ARXIV/docs/a4-phanes-axis.md.

children.detail: An instrumented 'expected rounds to saturation' or 'novelty decay rate' closed form could become a hexa verify --expr atom in a future engine-instrumentation lane (also ties to metering/billing-basis — 'billed per OUROBOROS round').

children.name: This is engine work, not arxiv-citation math, so
children.detail: This is engine work, not arxiv-citation math, so it does NOT count as an A4 verify; quantifying it upstream would go via hexa-lang inbox/patches (t_downstream_discipline).

children.name: ⚖️ LATTICE_POLICY — real-limits-first verification (cross-project)
children.detail: Cross-project policy (scope: all dancinlab projects + future repos), referenced by commons g25 + g26. ONE LINE: a project's ceiling is set by real mathematical/physical/engineering limits, NOT by the n=6 lattice — the lattice is a TOOL, not a CONSTRAINT. Folded from LATTICE_POLICY.md.

children.name: For phanes this grounds the honest-scope contract
children.detail: For phanes this grounds the honest-scope contract: a discovery is judged against real falsifiers (the tenant verifier + real thresholds), never a self-consistent tautology.

children.name: The two lattice usages
children.detail: Two ways a project uses the n=6 lattice, plus the dishonest anti-patterns.

children.name: Native invariant = VALID
children.detail: Native invariant (project uses n=6 as an explicit design invariant, e.g. isa_n6/hexa1/npu_n6) = VALID.

children.name: Self-imposed ceiling = INVALID
children.detail: Self-imposed ceiling (using 'must be n=6-compatible' as a constraint) = INVALID artificial bottleneck.

children.name: Anti-patterns (all dishonest)
children.detail: Anti-patterns (all dishonest): fit-to-convenient-number, arithmetic disguised as a physical limit, tautology (always-passes = zero verification power), external over-claim.

children.name: The real ceiling — math · physics · engineering
children.detail: The real ceiling, by domain of limit.

children.name: Mathematical
children.detail: Mathematical: Shannon entropy H=−Σ p log p · Kolmogorov lower bound · computability (Halting/Rice) · Bekenstein bound · statistical power · PAC-learning.

children.name: Physical
children.detail: Physical: c · ℏ · k (Landauer) · Stefan-Boltzmann · Carnot · Bremermann ·

children.name: Margolus-Levitin · Bekenstein-Hawking
children.detail: Margolus-Levitin · Bekenstein-Hawking.

children.name: Engineering
children.detail: Engineering: fab throughput · grid capacity · launch cost/kg · permit/funding/labor envelopes · patent thicket — each from the entity's OWN published data.

children.name: Verification standard + application tiers + operator stance
children.detail: The verification standard, the application tiers, and the operator stance.

children.name: STANDARD
children.detail: STANDARD: no lattice anchor alone (never a lattice tautology as a sole HARD check); real-limit anchor first (≥1 limit from §2); falsifiers on real thresholds; no external over-claim.

children.name: TIERS
children.detail: TIERS: lattice-native (n=6 is spec) · lattice-acceptor (own domain physics limits) · external envelope (NO lattice HARD check / χ²-falsifier; disclose 'n=6 is our framing').

children.name: OPERATOR STANCE
children.detail: OPERATOR STANCE: the first question is NOT 'how does this fit n=6?' but 'what is this domain's REAL invariant?'; use the lattice only if it appears naturally.
